name: S-band
typicalUse: TT&C, weather radar, some mobile satcom
notes: Traditional TT&C workhorse; reliable in adverse weather

name: C-band
notes: Widely deployed for fixed satcom; 5G spectrum sharing concerns

name: X-band
typicalUse: Military satcom, Earth observation downlink, deep-space
notes: Primary EO data downlink band; government/military allocations

name: Ku-band
typicalUse: DTH television, VSAT broadband, TDRS relay
notes: High throughput but rain-fade susceptible above ~14 GHz

name: Ka-band
notes: Very high data rates possible; requires rain-fade mitigation (ACM, site diversity)

name: V-band
typicalUse: Experimental high-capacity feeder links, inter-satellite links
notes: 60 GHz oxygen absorption peak makes some sub-bands ISL-only; emerging for NGSO feeder links
